page: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Skylake architecture operating at 2.0 GHz. It features 4 shared vCPUs across 4 physical cores, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25 percent at 2411.16. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present some limitations. Multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range, while uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths are weak, scoring in the bottom 25 percent at 35.47 GB/sec and 17.49 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and database operations also show weaker performance, with PassMark database operations scoring 2003.37 and static web serving throughput reaching 5.46 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1 to 4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded performance but do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. The bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD adds storage density value. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, light web serving, Redis caching, and basic single-threaded processing tasks, though it is less optimal for heavy database operations or large-scale multi-threaded workloads.
description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring an Intel Skylake processor with 4 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25 percent on stress-ng benchmarks. However, multi-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth are average to weak, with large-block memory writes and database operations showing lower efficiency. The shared CPU allocation and balanced resource ratio make this instance a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high single-threaded speed and local NVMe storage without the need for dedicated multi-core resources. It is best suited for general-purpose computing, Redis caching, and light web serving.
og_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is an Intel-based server with 4 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining strong single-core processing with fast local NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Skylake architecture with 4 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25 percent of benchmarks
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Well-suited for Redis caching, light web serving, and general-purpose tasks
